title: Fixing Broken HTML Validation with Strict Tag Names

statement.markdown: Given a string representing an HTML document, validate whether all HTML tags have valid and strict names. A valid tag name is one that starts with a letter (A-Z or a-z) and only contains letters (A-Z or a-z), digits (0-9), hyphens (-), underscores (_), and forward slashes (/). However, for this task, we will only allow letters, hyphens, and underscores. Tag names are case-insensitive. If the HTML document is valid, return 'Valid HTML'; otherwise, return the first invalid tag name found.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing a potential HTML document.
statement.outputFormat: A string indicating whether the HTML document is valid or the first invalid tag name found.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input string only contains ASCII characters and does not contain any nested tags.

constraints.description: The maximum length of an HTML tag name.

examples.explanation: The tag 'b' is not valid because it contains a character other than a letter, a hyphen, or an underscore.

hints.text: First hint text: Write a function to extract and validate each tag name from the input string.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Iterate over the characters in the input string, and for each tag name, check if it starts with a letter and only contains letters, hyphens, and underscores. If a tag name is invalid, return it immediately.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Iterate over all possible tag names and check if they are valid. However, this approach is inefficient and may not pass all test cases.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a regular expression to extract and validate tag names.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Iterate over the characters in the input string, and for each tag name, use a regular expression to check if it is valid. If a tag name is invalid, return it immediately.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The optimal solution correctly checks each tag name according to the given rules and returns the first invalid tag name found.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Test the optimal solution with the given examples to verify its correctness.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Not handling case insensitivity correctly.
Mistake 2: Not checking for tag name length correctly.

starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, s: str) -> str: pass

buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(s) { let tags = s.match(/<[^>]*>/g); for (let i = 0; i < tags.length; i++) { let tag = tags[i].slice(1, -1); if (tag[0] >= 'a' && tag[0] <= 'z') { if (/[0-9]/.test(tag)) { return 'Invalid tag: ' + tag; } } else { if (/[0-9]/.test(tag)) { return 'Invalid tag: ' + tag; } } } return 'Valid HTML'; }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, s: str) -> str: tags = [t.strip('<>') for t in s.split('<')[1:]]; for tag in tags: if tag[0].islower() and any(c not in 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz_/-' for c in tag): return 'Invalid tag: ' + tag; return 'Valid HTML';

solutions.javascript: // Solution: Use a regular expression to extract and validate tag names.
